label: Formula 13.15
subsection: Reducing Environmental Noise: Stratified Mass Selection
nearby_text: The relative advantage of stratification (assuming the block size is modest to large, so that $ 1 - 1/n \simeq 1 $) is $$ \sqrt{\frac{\sigma_{G}^{2}+\sigma_{B}^{2}+\sigma_{e}^{2}}{\sigma_{G}^{2}+\sigma_{e}^{2}}}=\sqrt{1+\frac{\sigma_{B}^{2}}{\sigma_{G}^{2}+\sigma_{e}^{2}}} $$ (13.15)

label: Formula 13.16a
nearby_text: Our analysis depends on the repeatability (LW Chapter 6) of the trait. The character value for the jth measure of individual i is decomposed as $$ z_{ij}=G_{i}+E_{i}+e_{ij} $$ (13.16a) where $ G_i $ and $ E_i $ are the genotypic and (permanent) environmental values common to all measures of $ i $, and $ e_{ij} $ is the special environmental value restricted to the $ j $th measure o

label: Formula 13.17a
nearby_text: the accuracy in using $ \overline{z}_{i} $ to predict $ A_{i} $, we need both the covariance between $ \overline{z}_{i} $ and $ A_{i} $, and the variance of $ \overline{z}_{i} $. The former is simply $$ \sigma(A_{i},\overline{z}_{i})=\sigma\left(A_{i},\frac{1}{n}\sum_{j=1}^{n}z_{ij}\right)=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{j=1}^{n}\sigma(A_{i},z_{ij})=\frac{1}{n}n\sigma(A_{i},A_{i})=\sigma_{A}^{2} $$ (13.17a)

  "nearby_text": "the accuracy in using $ \\overline{z}_{i} $ to predict $ A_{i} $, we need both the covariance between $ \\overline{z}_{i} $ and $ A_{i} $, and the variance of $ \\overline{z}_{i} $. The former is simply $$ \\sigma(A_{i},\\overline{z}_{i})=\\sigma\\left(A_{i},\\frac{1}{n}\\sum_{j=1}^{n}z_{ij}\\right)=\\frac{1}{n}\\sum_{j=1}^{n}\\sigma(A_{i},z_{ij})=\\frac{1}{n}n\\sigma(A_{i},A_{i})=\\sigma_{A}^{2} $$ (13.17a)"
}

label: Formula 13.17b
nearby_text: To obtain the variance of $ \overline{z}_{i} $, starting with $$ \overline{z}_{i}=\frac{1}{n}\sum_{j=1}^{n}z_{ij}=G_{i}+E_{i}+\frac{1}{n}\sum_{j=1}^{n}e_{ij} $$ (13.17b) $$ \begin{aligned}\sigma^{2}\left(\overline{z}_{i}\right)&=\sigma_{G}^{2}+\sigma_{E}^{2}+\sigma_{e}^{2}/n\\&=\sigma_{z}^{2}r+\sigma_{z}^{2}\frac{1-r}{n}=\sigma_{z}^{2}\left(\frac{1+(n-1)r}{n}\right)\

  "nearby_text": "To obtain the variance of $ \\overline{z}_{i} $, starting with $$ \\overline{z}_{i}=\\frac{1}{n}\\sum_{j=1}^{n}z_{ij}=G_{i}+E_{i}+\\frac{1}{n}\\sum_{j=1}^{n}e_{ij} $$ (13.17b) $$ \\begin{aligned}\\sigma^{2}\\left(\\overline{z}_{i}\\right)&=\\sigma_{G}^{2}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma_{e}^{2}/n\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}r+\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\frac{1-r}{n}=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\left(\\frac{1+(n-1)r}{n}\\right)\\end{aligned} $$ (13.17c) it immediately follows from Equation 13.16c that"
}

label: Formula 13.18a
nearby_text: The resulting accuracy in using $ \overline{z}_{i} $ to predict $ A_{i} $ becomes $$ \rho(\overline{z}_{i},A_{i})=\frac{\sigma(A_{i},\overline{z}_{i})}{\sigma_{A}\sigma(\overline{z}_{i})}=\frac{\sigma_{A}^{2}}{\sigma_{A}\sqrt{\sigma_{z}^{2}\left(\frac{1+(n-1)r}{n}\right)}}=h\sqrt{\frac{n}{1+(n-1)r}} $$ (13.18a) giving the response as $$ R=\bar{\imath}\rho(\overline{z}_{i},A_{i})\sigma_{A}=\bar{\imath}h\sqrt{\frac{n}{1+(n-1)r}}\sigma_{A} $$ (13.18b)

label: Formula 13.20
nearby_text: Assuming that the variance components are independent of age and sex, the yearly rate of response, $ r_{y} $, can be expressed as $$ r_{y}=\left(\frac{\bar{\imath}_{s}+\bar{\imath}_{d}}{L_{s}+L_{d}}\right)h^{2}\sigma_{z}=\left(\frac{\bar{\imath}_{s}+\bar{\imath}_{d}}{L_{s}+L_{d}}\right)h\sigma_{A} $$ (13.20) where $ \bar{i}_{s} $ and $ \bar{i}_{d} $ denote the selection intensities of the sire (father) and dam (mother). This result (in a slightly different form) is from Rendel and Robertson (1950), altho

label: Formula 13.22a
nearby_text: trength of response one needs some sort of standardization. One obvious approach is to express the response in units of phenotypic standard deviations (variance-standardization). label: Formula 13.24a
nearby_text: lly in Volume 3, we can gain significant insight by considering the simple case of two traits, both of which are potentially under selection. Equation 13.23b gives the expected vector of responses as $$ \mathbf{R}=\begin{pmatrix}R_{1}\\ R_{2}\end{pmatrix}=\mathbf{G}\mathbf{P}^{-1}\mathbf{S}=\begin{pmatrix}G_{11}&G_{12}\\ G_{12}&G_{22}\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}P_{11}&P_{12}\\ P_{12}&P_{22}\end{pmatrix}^{-1}\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}\\ S_{2}\end{pmatrix} $$ (13.24a)

  "nearby_text": "tion 8.11 to compute the inverse of P, and, recalling for a covariance that $ P_{12} = \\rho_{z} \\sqrt{P_{11} P_{22}} $, where $ \\rho_{z} $ is the phenotypic correlation between the two traits, yields $$ \\begin{aligned}\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}-P_{12}^{2}}\\begin{pmatrix}P_{22}&-P_{12}\\\\ -P_{12}&P_{11}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}\\\\ S_{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\\\&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\ -S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{pmatrix}\\end{aligned} $$ (13.24b)"

label: Formula 13.24c
nearby_text: Substituting into Equation 13.24a and recalling that $ h_{i}^{2} = G_{ii} / P_{ii} $, the response in trait one becomes $$ \begin{aligned}R_{1}&=\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\rho_{z}^{2})}\left(G_{11}\quad G_{12}\right)\left(\begin{aligned}&S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\&-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\end{aligned}\right)\\&=\frac{G_{11}\left(S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\right)+G_{12}\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\rho_{z}^{2})}\\&=\frac{h_{1}^{2}}{\left(1-\rho_{z}^{2}\right)}\left(S_{1}-S_{2}\frac{P_{12}}{P_{22}}\right)+\frac{G_{12}\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\rho_{z}^{2})}\end{aligned} $$ (13.24c) with an analogous expression for $ R_{2} $. The breeder's equation is recovered only when trait one is phenotypically $ (P_{12} = \rho_{z} = 0) $ and genetically $ (G_{12} = 0) $ uncorrelated with tr

      {
        "id": "formula_13.24a",
        "latex": "\\mathbf{R}=\\begin{pmatrix}R_{1}\\\\ R_{2}\\end{pmatrix}=\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}=\\begin{pmatrix}G_{11}&G_{12}\\\\ G_{12}&G_{22}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}P_{11}&P_{12}\\\\ P_{12}&P_{22}\\end{pmatrix}^{-1}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}\\\\ S_{2}\\end{pmatrix}",

      {
        "id": "formula_13.24b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}-P_{12}^{2}}\\begin{pmatrix}P_{22}&-P_{12}\\\\ -P_{12}&P_{11}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}\\\\ S_{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\\\&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\ -S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{pmatrix}\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 13.24b",
        "section": "THE MULTIVARIATE BREEDER'S EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Response With Two Traits",
        "position": 60,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_13.24c",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R_{1}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\left(G_{11}\\quad G_{12}\\right)\\left(\\begin{aligned}&S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\&-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{aligned}\\right)\\\\&=\\frac{G_{11}\\left(S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\right)+G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\\\&=\\frac{h_{1}^{2}}{\\left(1-\\rho_{z}^{2}\\right)}\\left(S_{1}-S_{2}\\frac{P_{12}}{P_{22}}\\right)+\\frac{G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 13.24c",
        "section": "THE MULTIVARIATE BREEDER'S EQUATION",

The interpretation of $ \\beta_i $ is that it represents the change in relative fitness given a one unit change in trait"

        "latex": "\\sigma(A_{i},\\overline{z}_{i})=\\sigma\\left(A_{i},\\frac{1}{n}\\sum_{j=1}^{n}z_{ij}\\right)=\\frac{1}{n}\\sum_{j=1}^{n}\\sigma(A_{i},z_{ij})=\\frac{1}{n}n\\sigma(A_{i},A_{i})=\\sigma_{A}^{2}",

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\sigma^{2}\\left(\\overline{z}_{i}\\right)&=\\sigma_{G}^{2}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma_{e}^{2}/n\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}r+\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\frac{1-r}{n}=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\left(\\frac{1+(n-1)r}{n}\\right)\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 13.17c",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_13.18a",
        "latex": "\\rho(\\overline{z}_{i},A_{i})=\\frac{\\sigma(A_{i},\\overline{z}_{i})}{\\sigma_{A}\\sigma(\\overline{z}_{i})}=\\frac{\\sigma_{A}^{2}}{\\sigma_{A}\\sqrt{\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\left(\\frac{1+(n-1)r}{n}\\right)}}=h\\sqrt{\\frac{n}{1+(n-1)r}}",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_13.23b",
        "latex": "\\mathbf{R}=\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}",

      {
        "id": "formula_13.23c",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_13.24a",
        "latex": "\\mathbf{R}=\\begin{pmatrix}R_{1}\\\\ R_{2}\\end{pmatrix}=\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}=\\begin{pmatrix}G_{11}&G_{12}\\\\ G_{12}&G_{22}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}P_{11}&P_{12}\\\\ P_{12}&P_{22}\\end{pmatrix}^{-1}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}\\\\ S_{2}\\end{pmatrix}",
        "label": "Formula 13.24a",

        "position": 59,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_13.24b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}-P_{12}^{2}}\\begin{pmatrix}P_{22}&-P_{12}\\\\ -P_{12}&P_{11}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}\\\\ S_{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\\\&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\ -S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{pmatrix}\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 13.24b",
        "section": "THE MULTIVARIATE BREEDER'S EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Response With Two Traits",
        "position": 60,
        "symbols_defined": [
          "\\mathbf{P}",
          "\\mathbf{S}"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_13.24c",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R_{1}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\left(G_{11}\\quad G_{12}\\right)\\left(\\begin{aligned}&S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\&-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{aligned}\\right)\\\\&=\\frac{G_{11}\\left(S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\right)+G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\\\&=\\frac{h_{1}^{2}}{\\left(1-\\rho_{z}^{2}\\right)}\\left(S_{1}-S_{2}\\frac{P_{12}}{P_{22}}\\right)+\\frac{G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 13.24c",
        "section": "THE MULTIVARIATE BREEDER'S EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Response With Two Traits",
        "position": 61,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_13.25a",
        "latex": "\\boldsymbol{\\beta}=\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}",

label: Formula 13.26b
subsection: Accounting for Genetic Correlations: The Lande Equation
nearby_text: n (Lande 1979a), $$ \mathbf{R}=\mathbf{G}\boldsymbol{\beta} $$ (13.26a) which is the multivariate version of Equation 13.8c, $ R = \sigma_A^2 \beta $. Considering two traits, using the Lande equation $$ \mathbf{R}=\begin{pmatrix}R_{1}\\ R_{2}\end{pmatrix}=\mathbf{G}\boldsymbol{\beta}=\begin{pmatrix}G_{11}&G_{12}\\ G_{12}&G_{22}\end{pmatrix}\begin{pmatrix}\beta_{1}\\ \beta_{2}\end{pmatrix} $$ (13.26b) yields greatly simplified expressions (relative to Equation 13.24c) for the responses $$ R_{1}=G_{11}\beta_{1}+G_{12}\beta_{2} $$ (13.26c) $$ R_{2}=G_{12}\beta_{1}+G_{22}\beta_{2} $$

        "label": "Formula 13.24b",
        "section": "THE MULTIVARIATE BREEDER'S EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Response With Two Traits",

      {
        "id": "formula_13.24c",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R_{1}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\left(G_{11}\\quad G_{12}\\right)\\left(\\begin{aligned}&S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\&-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{aligned}\\right)\\\\&=\\frac{G_{11}\\left(S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\right)+G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\\\&=\\frac{h_{1}^{2}}{\\left(1-\\rho_{z}^{2}\\right)}\\left(S_{1}-S_{2}\\frac{P_{12}}{P_{22}}\\right)+\\frac{G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\end{aligned}",

subsection: Accounting for Genetic Correlations: The Lande Equation
nearby_text: two traits are genetically uncorrelated, selection on one has no impact on the response of the other, even if they are phenotypically correlated. More generally, with n potentially correlated traits $$ R_{i}=G_{ii}\beta_{i}+\sum_{j\neq i}G_{ij}\beta_{j} $$ (13.26d)

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\sigma^{2}\\left(\\overline{z}_{i}\\right)&=\\sigma_{G}^{2}+\\sigma_{E}^{2}+\\sigma_{e}^{2}/n\\\\&=\\sigma_{z}^{2}r+\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\frac{1-r}{n}=\\sigma_{z}^{2}\\left(\\frac{1+(n-1)r}{n}\\right)\\end{aligned}",

      {
        "id": "formula_13.24a",
        "latex": "\\mathbf{R}=\\begin{pmatrix}R_{1}\\\\ R_{2}\\end{pmatrix}=\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}=\\begin{pmatrix}G_{11}&G_{12}\\\\ G_{12}&G_{22}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}P_{11}&P_{12}\\\\ P_{12}&P_{22}\\end{pmatrix}^{-1}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}\\\\ S_{2}\\end{pmatrix}",

      {
        "id": "formula_13.24b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}-P_{12}^{2}}\\begin{pmatrix}P_{22}&-P_{12}\\\\ -P_{12}&P_{11}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}\\\\ S_{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\\\&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\ -S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{pmatrix}\\end{aligned}",

        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R_{1}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\left(G_{11}\\quad G_{12}\\right)\\left(\\begin{aligned}&S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\&-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{aligned}\\right)\\\\&=\\frac{G_{11}\\left(S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\right)+G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\\\&=\\frac{h_{1}^{2}}{\\left(1-\\rho_{z}^{2}\\right)}\\left(S_{1}-S_{2}\\frac{P_{12}}{P_{22}}\\right)+\\frac{G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\end{aligned}",

      {
        "id": "formula_13.24c",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R_{1}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\left(G_{11}\\quad G_{12}\\right)\\left(\\begin{aligned}&S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\&-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{aligned}\\right)\\\\&=\\frac{G_{11}\\left(S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\right)+G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\\\&=\\frac{h_{1}^{2}}{\\left(1-\\rho_{z}^{2}\\right)}\\left(S_{1}-S_{2}\\frac{P_{12}}{P_{22}}\\right)+\\frac{G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\end{aligned}",

label: Formula 13.29
subsection: Selection Gradients and Mean Population Fitness
nearby_text: ter 6). If the fitness determined by the vector of traits, z, can be expressed as a linear regression (Equation 13.25b), then the expected change in fitness from selection response on these traits is $$ \Delta\overline{w}=\beta^{T}\Delta\mathbf{z}=\beta^{T}\mathbf{R}=\beta^{T}\mathbf{G}\beta $$ (13.29) From Equation 13.28b, this is just the additive variance in fitness associated with these traits. Thus, we recover Fisher's theorem that the change in mean fitness equals the additive variance in rel

      {
        "id": "formula_13.24a",
        "latex": "\\mathbf{R}=\\begin{pmatrix}R_{1}\\\\ R_{2}\\end{pmatrix}=\\mathbf{G}\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}=\\begin{pmatrix}G_{11}&G_{12}\\\\ G_{12}&G_{22}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}P_{11}&P_{12}\\\\ P_{12}&P_{22}\\end{pmatrix}^{-1}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}\\\\ S_{2}\\end{pmatrix}",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_13.24b",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}\\mathbf{P}^{-1}\\mathbf{S}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}-P_{12}^{2}}\\begin{pmatrix}P_{22}&-P_{12}\\\\ -P_{12}&P_{11}\\end{pmatrix}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}\\\\ S_{2}\\end{pmatrix}\\\\&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\begin{pmatrix}S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\ -S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{pmatrix}\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 13.24b",
        "section": "THE MULTIVARIATE BREEDER'S EQUATION",
        "subsection": "Response With Two Traits",

        "symbols_defined": [

          "\\mathbf{S}"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_13.24c",
        "latex": "\\begin{aligned}R_{1}&=\\frac{1}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\left(G_{11}\\quad G_{12}\\right)\\left(\\begin{aligned}&S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\\\&-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\end{aligned}\\right)\\\\&=\\frac{G_{11}\\left(S_{1}P_{22}-S_{2}P_{12}\\right)+G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\\\&=\\frac{h_{1}^{2}}{\\left(1-\\rho_{z}^{2}\\right)}\\left(S_{1}-S_{2}\\frac{P_{12}}{P_{22}}\\right)+\\frac{G_{12}\\left(-S_{1}P_{12}+S_{2}P_{11}\\right)}{P_{11}P_{22}(1-\\rho_{z}^{2})}\\end{aligned}",
        "label": "Formula 13.24c",

  "nearby_text": "ter 6). If the fitness determined by the vector of traits, z, can be expressed as a linear regression (Equation 13.25b), then the expected change in fitness from selection response on these traits is $$ \\Delta\\overline{w}=\\beta^{T}\\Delta\\mathbf{z}=\\beta^{T}\\mathbf{R}=\\beta^{T}\\mathbf{G}\\beta $$ (13.29) From Equation 13.28b, this is just the additive variance in fitness associated with these traits. Thus, we recover Fisher's theorem that the change in mean fitness equals the additive variance in rel"
